free_text: The Capture Cross Section of Boron for Neutrons of
Energies from 0.01 ev to 1000 ev
Journal publication.
Original publication.
Method of
modulation of a cyclotron beam.
Translated from SCISRS.
Converted to reaction formalism
BP: Updated to new date formats, lower
case. Corrections according last EXFOR rules and
Dict. Digitized transmission and cross section data.
Read from curve for BNL-325.
Compiler's comment: Cannot trace these data in
original publications. Perhaps, they were combined from
LA-92 cross section measurements.
Absorber #1: 0.0234 g/cm2 of B and 0.123 g/cm2 of F.
Neutron energies were determined by using the
method of modulation of a cyclotron beam to obtain
the time of Right of the neutrons over a 7.6-meter
path between the neutron moderator and a BF3 neutron
detector. Resolution (on-time) 200 microsec,
frequency 100 cps, source boron, collimation Type 1,
filter none.
Possibly the greatest source of error in
this experiment is the determination of the boron
content of the absorbers.
Fig. 2, page 13 of report LA-92,1944.
BP
Absorber #2: 0.1493 g/cm2 of B, 0.0735 g/cm2 of C.
Neutron energies were determined by using the
method of modulation of a cyclotron beam to obtain
the time of Right of the neutrons over a 7.6-meter
path between the neutron moderator and a BF3 neutron
detector. Resolution (on-time) 50 microsec,
frequency 1000 cps, source paraffin,
collimation Type 1, Cd filter.
Possibly the greatest source of error in
this experiment is the determination of the boron
content of the absorbers.
Fig. 3, page 14 of report LA-92,1944.

Absorber #3: 0.594 g/cm2 of B, 0.285 g/cm2 of C,
0.049 g/cm2 of O, 0.17 g/cm2 of Pb.
Neutron energies were determined by using the
method of modulation of a cyclotron beam to obtain
the time of Right of the neutrons over a 7.6-meter
path between the neutron moderator and a BF3 neutron
detector. Resolution (on-time) 10 microsec,

Possibly the greatest source of error in

Absorber #4: 1.80 g/cm2 of B, 0.885 g/cm2 of C.
Neutron energies were determined by using the
method of modulation of a cyclotron beam to obtain
the time of Right of the neutrons over a 7.6-meter
path between the neutron moderator and a BF3 neutron

Fig. 5, page 16 of report LA-92,1944.
BP
These cross-sections include the scattering
cross-sections of boron and the elements other than
boron which were present in the absorbers. To extract
cross sections one can use a simple formula:
Transmission = exp(-sigma*n), where sigma is cross
section and n is the number of atoms in absorber.
Possibly the greatest source of error in
this experiment is the determination of the boron
content of the absorbers.
Fig. 6, page 17 of report LA-92,1944.

BP
Table of assumed
cross sections.
The scattering cross section is obtained by taking
the extrapolated value of the total cross section at
zero time of flight. Since these scattering
cross-sections include the scattering of elements
other than boron, in order to determine the boron
scattering, it is necessary to subtract the
contribution of the other elements to the scattering.
page 9 of report LA-92,1944.
